headline: Youtube Inspires Entrepreneur To Explore Furniture Industry
article_summary: PENAMPANG(Sabah) (Bernama) – The saying ‘As one door closes, another opens’ fits Lokman Awaluddin to a T.

headline: Terengganu floods: number of evacuees continues to rise
article_summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, Nov 25  -- Kemaman became the latest district to be hit by floods  in Terengganu with the number of evacuees in the state continue to increase to 690 people from 184 families as at 8 am today compared to 389 people (94 families) recorded last night.

headline: Bursa Malaysia opens higher, in line with global stocks
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Bursa Malaysia opened on a higher note today, in line with positive development of global stocks on better economic forecast.

headline: Gold futures open untraded
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- The gold futures contract on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives opened untraded today.

headline: 199 flood victims at relief centres in Melaka
article_summary: MELAKA, Nov 25  -- The number of flood victims at three evacuation centres in Melaka remained at 199 people from 48 families as of 8 am today.

headline: KLCI futures higher at opening
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI futures contract on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives traded higher this morning, tracking the upward momentum in the underlying cash market.

headline: Water supply to 27 areas in Kuala Langat, Sepang restored in stages
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- The repair work to replace the valve at the distribution pipes at the intersection of Jalan Indah 1, Jalan B48 Pekan Sepang Lama has been completed earlier than scheduled at 3 am today.

headline: Pakistan denies reports on recognition of Israel
article_summary: KARACHI, Nov 25 -- Pakistan on Tuesday dismissed as “baseless” speculation regarding the possibility of its recognition of Israel, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: Support Budget 2021, don't make people become political victims - PEMANIS
article_summary: KANGAR 25 Nov -- The Perlis Malay Association (PEMANIS) today called on all parliamentary members (MPs), particularly the Malay leaders, to  support the 2021 budget, which is expected to be decided by vote in Parliament tomorrow so that the people do not become “political victims”.

headline: Vietnam PM approves proposal on national tourism conference
article_summary: HANOI, Nov 25 -- Vietnam’s Prime Minister Nguyen Xuan Phuc has approved a proposal on organising a national conference on tourism submitted by the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism and the People’s Committee of central Quang Nam province.

headline: Zynga partners Liberty Walk on design competition in CSR Racing 2
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Global leader in interactive entertainment, Zynga has announced partnership with Japanese premier car tuner Liberty Walk for the launch of the new Elite Tuners feature and ‘Design A Wrap’ competition in CSR Racing 2 (CSR2).

headline: Slight increase in flood victims in Kuala Krai
article_summary: KOTA BHARU,  Nov 25 -- The number of flood victims at the two relief centres in Kuala Krai increased to 14 people, involving three families this morning, from only six people (two families) yesterday.

headline: Azerbaijani army enters Kalbajar region after 27 years
article_summary: BAKU, Nov 25 -- The Azerbaijani army entered the Kalbajar region following 27 years of Armenian occupation, reported Anadolu Agency, quoting the country’s Defence Ministry early Wednesday.

headline: Israel missile attack hits Syrian military targets
article_summary: DAMASCUS, Nov 25 -- An Israeli missile attack hit military sites in the southern countryside of the capital Damascus on Tuesday night, causing damage only, reported Xinhua news agency.

headline: Japan excludes Sapporo, Osaka from travel subsidies for three weeks
article_summary: TOKYO, Nov 25 -- Japan will exclude Sapporo and Osaka from its domestic travel subsidy campaign for three weeks starting Tuesday due to a recent resurgence of coronavirus cases in the two popular tourist destinations, the government said.

headline: Biden says transition process 'not so far behind curve' after talks with Trump officials
article_summary: WASHINGTON, Nov 25 -- Projected President-elect Joe Biden said the transition process is not as far behind as once feared despite the delay in accessing resources and communicating with Trump administration officials, reported Sputnik news agency.

headline: Kedah floods: 85 evacuated from three districts
article_summary: ALOR SETAR, Nov 25  -- A total of 85 people from three districts in Kedah were evacuated to three temporary relief centres (PPS) after their houses were flooded following heavy rain on Monday.

headline: Barcelona qualify for Champions League last 16
article_summary: ANKARA, Nov 25 -- Spanish football powerhouse Barcelona booked their place in the UEFA Champions League Round of 16 with a 4-0 win Tuesday against Ukraine's Dynamo Kyiv, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: BTS gets Grammy nod in another landmark feat
article_summary: WASHINGTON/SEOUL, Nov 25 -- South Korea's K-pop megastar BTS was nominated for the Grammy Award in best pop duo/group performance, becoming the first South Korean pop artist to be nominated for the US music award, Yonhap news agency reported.

headline: Can UBI concept be the saviour during COVID-19 economic fallout?
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Amidst the social pandemonium and economic fallout caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, a local academician believes a rather revolutionary and unorthodox concept that was once tried out in Canada seems worth a relook - the universal basic income (UBI) concept.

headline: Afghan donor conference pledges US$12 bln over four years
article_summary: GENEVA, Nov 25 -- Afghanistan received pledges Tuesday from countries and international organisations of US$3 billion in development support for 2021 with a total of US$12 billion (US$1=RM4.1) offered over four years at a conference held at the United Nations in Geneva, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: Preparations for 4th World Nomad Games discussed in Turkey’s Istanbul
article_summary: BISHKEK, Nov 25 -- A preparations for the IV World Nomad Games were discussed in Istanbul, the press service of Kyrgyzstan’s Ministry of Culture, Information and Tourism reported.

headline: UN chief urges Ethiopia to protect civilians in Tigray region - Spokesman
article_summary: NEW YORK, Nov 25 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres urged Ethiopia to do its utmost to protect civilians in the Tigray region, reported Sputnik news agency.

headline: Cooperation agreement on child protection signed in Vietnam
article_summary: HANOI, Nov 25 -- A cooperation agreement on child protection was signed on Tuesday between the Department of Child Affairs under the Ministry of Labour, Invalids and Social Affairs and Vietnam Post.

headline: IBRC strengthens expertise, technology for benefit of local manufacturers
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, 25 Nov -- SIRIM Berhad, through its Industrial Biotechnology Research Centre (IBRC), will strengthen the agency's laboratory expertise and technology to ascertain the safety, as well as increase the quality, of food, medical and pharmaceutical products.

headline: TIV to advance 14 per cent to 570,000 in 2021 - CGS-CIMB
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- CGS-CIMB forecasts the total industry volume (TIV) for 2021 (2021F) to rise by 14 per cent to 570,000 driven by stronger demand for national brands on the back of new model launches such as Proton’s X50 and Perodua’s new compact SUV, D55L.

headline: Budget 2021: RM400 mln injection to empower COSS - Nanta
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The RM400 million injection for the Cooking Oil Price Stabilisation Scheme (COSS) through Budget 2021 is expected to have an impact on the B40 target groups, said Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi.

headline: Dewan Rakyat to sit until 3 pm today
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The Dewan Rakyat sitting today will be extended until 3 pm today, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Parliament and Law) Datuk Takiyuddin Hassan proposed today.

headline: Biden introduces Cabinet picks of his administration
article_summary: WASHINGTON, Nov 25 -- US President-elect Joe Biden introduced his Cabinet nominees and appointees Tuesday and said he is ready to confront the nation’s adversaries and not reject allies, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: US congratulates Malaysia for success in hosting APEC Summit
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The United States’ government congratulates Malaysia on its success in hosting the  Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) Summit, which saw the adoption of the Putrajaya Vision 2040 and the Kuala Lumpur Declaration, said Prime Minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.

headline: Bank Islam taps unbanked, underbanked micro-entrepreneurs with RM2 mln fund
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Bank Islam Malaysia Bhd today launched BangKIT Microfinance with an initial fund of RM2 million, a facility funded from donations made by the corporation and the public to assist the unbanked and underbanked micro-entrepreneurs.

headline: Sabah rural infrastructure projects on track
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Twenty-eight of the 101 rural road projects in Sabah under the 11th Malaysian Plan (2016-2020) have been completed and the rest are in various stages of implementation, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.

headline: Mexico: 113 bodies found in mass grave
article_summary: BOGOTA, Nov 25 -- Mexican authorities on Tuesday announced the discovery of a mass grave in the western state of Jalisco, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: Azerbaijan sets up coordination headquarters to address issues in liberated territories
article_summary: BAKU, Nov 25 -- Azerbaijan President Ilham Aliyev has signed an order on the establishment of a Coordination Headquarters for addressing the issues in the liberated territories of Azerbaijan in a centralised manner, Azerbaijan State News Agency (AZERTAC) reported.

headline: Netpoleon releases report on Business Email Compromise
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Netpoleon Solutions Pte Ltd (Netpoleon) has published the report ‘Business Email Compromise (BEC): How does it attack your business and how can you prevent it?’.

headline: Vietnamese coffee brand among nine winners of ASEAN-South Korea Design Award
article_summary: SEOUL, Nov 25 – Real Beans Coffee, a coffee brand in Vietnam, are among nine winners of the 1st ASEAN-South Korea Excellent Design Exhibition Award this year which honours the best packaging designs made in ASEAN countries.

headline: Stronger regional cooperation can help Asia, and the Pacific recovers from COVID-19
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Stronger regional cooperation strategies can help governments in Asia and the Pacific accelerate economic growth and a robust recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic, said the Asian Development Bank (ADB).

headline: Dak Nong Geopark in Vietnam earns global status
article_summary: DAK NONG, Nov 25 – The Central Highlands province of Dak Nong on November 24 received a certificate recognising its geopark as part of the UNESCO Global Geoparks Network.

headline: Bank Muamalat employee at Menara Bumiputra tests positive for COVID-19
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd (Bank Muamalat) has confirmed that one of its employees at its headquarters at Menara Bumiputra has tested positive for the COVID-19 virus and is now under the care of a government hospital.

headline: 18 remanded over probe into fatal border shooting incident
article_summary: KANGAR, Nov 25 -- Eighteen men have been remanded to facilitate investigations into the shootout between the General Operations Force (GOF) members and groups of smugglers at the Malaysia-Thailand border near Padang Besar yesterday.

headline: Mosques, surau in Sabah opened for prayers
article_summary: KOTA KINABALU,  Nov 25   -- Congregational prayers are now allowed to be performed in mosques and surau in all zones in Sabah effective today.

headline: Truckload of contraband alcoholic drinks headed from Labuan to  Sabah seized
article_summary: LABUAN, Nov 25 -- A ploy to fool enforcement authorities by concealing a consignment of contraband alcoholic drinks underneath stacks of wheat flour, on board a truck, failed when the Marine Police busted the attempt in an operation dubbed Op Terancang (Op Benteng COVID-19) yesterday.

headline: IGP hopes Parliament to approve 2021 Budget
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tan Sri Abdul Hamid Bador expressed the hope that the Dewan Rakyat will approve the 2021 budget to enable the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to maintain security and public order.

headline: Strict control over Covid at border camps in Thailand
article_summary: TAK, Nov 25 – Tak governor ordered strict control at border camps after an illegal migrant entered the Umpiem camp with Covid-19, Thai news agency (TNA) reported.

headline: KAFA wants names of four involved in cheating revealed
article_summary: KOTA BHARU, Nov 25 -- The FA of Kelantan (KAFA) has suggested that the management of  Kelantan FC reveal the names of four well-known individuals who were involved in a cheating case against team owner Norizam Tukiman.

headline: CPI declines 1.5 per cent y-o-y in October 2020 - DOSM
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Malaysia's Consumer Price Index (CPI) declined 1.5 per cent year-on-year (y-o-y) to 120.2 in October 2020 from 122.0 in the same month last year, said the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM).

headline: Niger’s former President Mamadou Tandja dies at 82
article_summary: NIGER, Nov 25 -- Niger’s former President Mamadou Tandja has died, the government announced Tuesday, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: 20 projects for upgrading of dilapidated schools, involving RM98.1 million, in Kota Belud
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- A  total of 20 projects for upgrading and rebuilding of dilapidated schools in Kota Belud, Sabah , have been approved, involving an allocation of RM98.1 million, said Deputy Education Minister I Muslimin Yahaya.

headline: UiTM denies terminating part-time lecturers' services
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) today denied reports claiming that its 600 part-time lecturers would have their services terminated, as reported by an online news portal yesterday.

headline: Mechanic finds python in car engine compartment
article_summary: JOHOR BAHRU, Nov 25 -- A mechanic saw more than the engine when he opened the bonnet of a car at a workshop in a village near Pontian last night.

headline: Islamic banking has great potential in social financial instruments - Mustapa
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Banking and financial institutions have a collective role to play in rebuilding the country after the devastation by COVID-19, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Economy), Datuk Seri Mustapa Mohamed.

headline: Pacific workers flown in to help fill Australia's agriculture labour gap
article_summary: SYDNEY, Nov 25 -- The impact of COVID-19 on overseas travel has left many Australian farmers short-handed during peak harvesting and processing seasons, reported Xinhua news agency.

headline: ASEAN needs to develop inclusive regional architecture for labour migration
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Member states of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) should take the opportunity to start developing a more inclusive regional architecture for labour migration within ASEAN.

headline: Vietnam-US defence policy dialogue held online
article_summary: HANOI, Nov 25 -- The 2020 Vietnam-US defence policy dialogue was held online on Tuesday, under the chair of Deputy Minister of Defence Sen. Lieut. Gen. Hoang Xuan Chien and US Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Indo-Pacific Security Affairs (IPSA) David F. Helvey.

headline: IATA calls for reopening of borders with testing, without quarantine
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The International Air Transport Association (IATA) at its 76th Annual General Meeting (AGM) unanimously resolved to urgently call on governments to re-open borders to travel.

headline: Malakoff plans to expand RE business, venture overseas  -- CGS-CIMB
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Malakoff Corporation Bhd aims to expand its waste management business by venturing into waste-to-energy (WTE), biogas and biomass power plants, with participation in the Large-Scale Solar 4 and Johor’s WTE plant bids this year.

headline: Queen: Families should stick together to break COVID-19 chain
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- The Raja Permaisuri Agong Tunku Hajah Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah today called on all Malaysians to look after their families, especially when the country is facing the COVID-19 pandemic.

headline: Jasa rebranded as J-KOM with different roles, functions - Saifuddin
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The government has agreed to rebrand the Department of Special Affairs (JASA) as the Department of Community Communications (J-KOM) with different roles and functions, Minister of Communications and Multimedia (KKMM) Datuk Saifuddin Abdullah said.

headline: Grab rakes in over RM130 mln in sales under SMO, ePENJANA initiatives
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Grab Malaysia has garnered more than RM130 million in sales to help local businesses stay afloat through the support of Shop Malaysia Online (SMO) and ePENJANA initiatives.

headline: Ecuador’s Interior Minister dismissed
article_summary: BOGOTA, Nov 25  -- Ecuador’s National Assembly censured and dismissed Interior Minister Maria Paula Romo on Tuesday for allowing harsh police intervention against thousands of anti-government protesters in October last year, Anadolu Agency reported.

headline: Terengganu  approves RM1.375 billion for state budget
article_summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, Nov 25  --  The Terengganu State Assembly unanimously passed the 2021 state budget today.

headline: Improving sentiment boosts emerging East Asian bond market amid COVID-19 -- ADB
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Improving sentiment has boosted the emerging East Asian bond markets amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

headline: Higher loans for B40 students at IPTS diploma level - Noraini
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Perbadanan Tabung Pendidikan Tinggi Nasional (PTPTN) loans for B40 students taking diploma courses at Private Institutions of Higher Learning (IPTS) will be increased from RM6,800 to RM8,000 per semester, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.

headline: Mah Sing Plastics inks RM12 mln PV deal with Plus Solar
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Mah Sing Plastics Industries Sdn Bhd (MSPI) has awarded a RM12 million solar photovoltaic (PV) installation project to clean energy provider Plus Solar Systems Sdn Bhd (Plus Solar) to outfit MSPI’s smart factory in Klang, Selangor.

headline: Business events barred, industry players burdened by high operating costs  - MACEOS
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- The pandemic and restrictions on face-to-face events have severely impacted the business events industry with revenue losses reported at 90 per cent compared to 2019.

headline: Police have reopened investigations into death of Teoh Beng Hock - KDN
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The police (PDRM) have reopened investigations into the death of Teoh Beng Hock who was found dead on July 16 2009, under Section 342 of the Penal Code (Wrongful Confinement).

headline: Higher unemployment rate in Terengganu due to COVID-19
article_summary: KUALA TERENGGANU,  Nov  25 -- The unemployment rate in Terengganu increased to 3.8 percent in the second quarter of this year, from 3.3 per cent in the first quarter, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the Terengganu State Assembly was told today.

headline: Sime Darby Property posts net loss of RM355.26 mln in Q3
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Sime Darby Property Bhd posted a net loss of RM355.26 million for the third quarter ended Sept 30, 2020 (Q320) against a net profit of RM25.24 million in the same period last year.

headline: Personnel conducting intelligence not required to wear bullefproof vest
article_summary: IPOH, Nov 25 -- The late Corporal Baharuddin Ramli and Corporal Norihan Tari who was badly injured in the shooting incident at the Malaysia-Thailand border early yesterday morning did not wear bulletproof vests because they were conducting an intelligence work and not patrolling.

headline: Corporal Baharuddin laid to rest
article_summary: JEMPOL, Nov 25  -- The late Corporal Baharuddin Ramli from 3rd Battalion (Senoi Praaq), Northern Brigade of the General Operations Force (GOF) who was killed while on duty at the Malaysia-Thailand border yesterday morning, was laid to rest at Kampung Sungai Sampo here at 12.20 pm today.

headline: More than 200 million people, including world leaders, call for world peace in virtual event
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov. 25  -- More than 200 million people from 194 countries, including world leaders, had gathered virtually on Sunday to call for world peace.

headline: Caged-fish breeders advised to apply for TOL
article_summary: KUALA TERENGGANU,  Nov 25   -- Caged-fish breeders who have built the cages on state land without permission are advised to apply for temporary occupation licence (TOL), before any enforcement action is taken.

headline: Two more Thais nabbed over shootout at Malaysian-Thai border - IGP
article_summary: JEMPOL, Nov 25 -- Two more Thais were arrested in Perlis today, bringing to eight the number of Thai nationals nabbed for the fatal shootout at the Malaysia-Thailand border yesterday, said Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Abdul Hamid Bador.

headline: Secret brief says UK may face several concurrent crises amid end of Brexit transition
article_summary: MOSCOW, Nov. 25  -- The United Kingdom is likely to face several high-level risks, including a bad flu season, sustained strikes and natural disasters as the Brexit transition period comes to a close, reported Sputnik news agency.

headline: Sarawak allocates RM190 mln to set up research centre for infectious diseases
article_summary: KOTA SAMARAHAN, Nov 25  -- The Sarawak government has allocated RM190 million to set up a research centre for infectious diseases, said Chief Minister Datuk Patinggi Abang Johari Tun Openg today.

headline: TDM returns to black in Q3 on higher CPO price
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- TDM Bhd returned to the black in the third quarter ended Sept 30, 2020, with a profit after tax of RM21.75 million against a loss after tax of RM41.4 million in the same quarter a year ago on higher crude palm oil (CPO) prices.

headline: Kyrgyzstan, China discuss possibility of opening flights between countries
article_summary: BISHKEK, Nov. 25 -- The government of Kyrgyzstan is interested in further building up trade, economic and investment cooperation with China, Acting Prime Minister, First Vice Prime Minister of the Kyrgyz Artem Novikov said during a meeting with Chinese Ambassador to Kyrgyzstan Du Dewen.

headline: Sukma: MSWP wants all three FTS to remain as one contingent
article_summary: PUTRAJAYA, Nov 25 -- The Federal Territories Sports Council (MSWP) does not intend to field three contingents for the Malaysia Games but all three federal territories to remain under one roof in the "spirit of the Federal Territories”.

headline: Monthly parking pass for EZ Smart Park app now available - DBKL
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov -- The monthly parking pass for the EZ Smart Park application have been activated since Nov 19.

headline: Nippon Express Global SCM (Shanghai) completes Qingpu Phase II Warehouse construction
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Nippon Express Global SCM (Shanghai) Co Ltd (NEGSS), a local subsidiary of Nippon Express Co Ltd of Tokyo, has completed construction of its Qingpu Phase II Warehouse, which opened for business recently.

headline: Sunway Malls bucks the trend with 200 shops opening despite pandemic
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Sunway Malls, which operates seven malls across Klang Valley, Penang and Johor, continues to see healthy demand for its retail space with the opening of 200 new shops across its malls despite the weak market condition arising from COVID-19 pandemic.

headline: Wonderful Tiffany gemstone lamps unveiled by The Secret Garden (TSG 1895)
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- The Secret Garden (TSG 1895) has presented a delectable collection of gemstone lamps and decorative accents inspired by the very first Tiffany lamp.

headline: Prominent indian politician Ahmed Patel dies at 71 after battling COVID-19
article_summary: NEW DELHI, Nov. 25 -- Prominent Indian politician Ahmed Patel died on Wednesday due to coronavirus-related complications at a hospital in Gurugram near New Delhi, Wednesday.

headline: French Parliament passes disputed global security bill to limit police images' publications
article_summary: MOSCOW, Nov. 25  -- French parliament late on Tuesday passed the bill on global security, criminalising the publication of images of police officers online, despite the recent riots against the legislation criticised for possible infringement on the freedom of the press, reported Sputnik news agency.

headline: Thai customs seizes 62 kg 'ice' destined for Malaysia
article_summary: BANGKOK, Nov. 25 -- Thai customs confiscated 62 kilogrammes of 'ice' (methamphetamine) in Bangkok worth about 37 million baht hidden in a shipping container destined for Malaysia.

headline: Malaysia's Leading Index increases 8.6 pct in Sept 2020 - DOSM
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Malaysia is expected to continue to chart economic recovery based on the smooth growth rate of the Leading Index (LI), which increased to 8.6 per cent to reach 109.5 points in September 2020 compared with 100.8 points in the same month in 2019, said the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM).

headline: Johor Police dispose contraband cigarettes, liquor worth RM2.5 million
article_summary: JOHOR BAHRU, Nov 25   --  Johor police today disposed of contraband cigarettes and liquor worth RM2.5 million which were seized from 2018 until October this year.

headline: AirAsia needs more than one round of fund raising, says CGS-CIMB Securities
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- CGS-CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd expects AirAsia Group Bhd’s (AAGB) one round of fund raising exercise will unlikely be enough, and the low-cost airline may need to raise more funds to keep its business afloat.

headline: COVID-19: Impose EMCO in Kulim to contain infection - MB
article_summary: ALOR SETAR, Nov 25 -- The Kedah government will submit an application for the Enhanced Movement Control Order (EMCO) to be implemented in Kulim, following the spread of a new cluster in the district.

headline: Maybank's web and app experiencing connection issue
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- Malayan Banking Bhd’s official website and mobile application, Maybank2u, are having connection issue on Wednesday afternoon.

headline: Riot police on standby at Siam Commercial Bank HQ
article_summary: BANGKOK, Nov 25  – Riot police are on standby outside the Siam Commercial Bank (SCB) head office on Ratchayothin Road as anti-government demonstrators are set to start their rally at 3 pm. on Wednesday.

headline: "Mom, where's dad?"
article_summary: KANGAR, Nov 25  -- "Mom, where's dad? We want to see him; we miss him!”

headline: Philippine campaign to attract more foreign investments, revive economy post-COVID-19
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR,  Nov. 25 -- The Philippines has launched the “Make It Happen in the Philippines” (MIHP) – a global campaign to attract foreign investments – as it aims for economic recovery amid the COVID-19 pandemic that had adversely hit economies worldwide.

headline: Media City project postponement will affect RTM operations - Saifuddin
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25 -- Communications and Multimedia Minister Datuk Saifuddin Abdullah today explained that the postponement of the Media City project will affect Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) as most of the equipment and systems used are under the scope of the project.

headline: S. Korea to mass produce advanced tactical ground-based missiles by 2025
article_summary: SEOUL, Nov 25 -- South Korea decided Wednesday to mass produce a new type of tactical ground-based missiles designed to destroy underground artillery bases in North Korea, Yonhap News Agency reported quoting officials.

headline: CMCO: 558 vehicles  attempting to enter Kelantan ordered to turn back - Police
article_summary: KOTA BHARU, Nov 25  -- A total of 558 vehicles attempting to enter Kelantan were prevented from entering the state and ordered to turn back since enforcement of the Conditional Movement Control Order (CMCO) in the red zone states since last Nov 9.

headline: Mior Faridalathrash's experience can transform Perak police contingent - Deputy IGP
article_summary: IPOH, Nov 25 -- The experience gained by Datuk Mior Faridalathrash Wahid who has been appointed as the new Perak police chief effective today can drive the state police contingent forward, ensuring that the Royal Malaysian Police (PDRM) remains a respected and powerful force.

headline: China company seeks approval for COVID-19 vaccine
article_summary: ANKARA, Nov 25  -- A leading Chinese company on Wednesday announced it has sought formal approval from authorities to bring its COVID-19 vaccine to the market, Anadolu Agency reported according to state-run media.

headline: Drug processing lab uncovered in Penang with arrest of eight
article_summary: GEORGE TOWN, Nov 25  -- Police have uncovered a drug processing laboratory, arrested eight men and seized drugs worth RM177,210 in special operations in Penang and Perak.

headline: Duration for ministries, depts and agencies to give feedback to PAC shortened
article_summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 25  -- The duration for all ministries, departments and agencies to respond to the Parliament's Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has now been shortened from three months to two, says PAC chairman Wong Kah Woh.
